project.name: OpenKrakey
project.description: Minimalist microkernel autonomous-agent framework (TypeScript/Node). Per-Agent: clock + event-system + orchestrator(+context-buffer) + loader, wrapped by agent_instance. Global: boot + cli.

core.description: The whole framework — per-Agent runtime modules + their L1 contracts + shared modules + the two global modules (boot, cli) + the LLM communication gateway.

plugins.description: Phase-1 MVP plugins under public_plugin/ — the essential set that makes a bare agent converse, remember, and use tools, exercising every core function. Each implements the L1 plugin contract and talks ONLY via the per-Agent event-system.

clock.description: Per-Agent dumb timer: countdown + activate; rhythm controllable.

event-system.description: Per-Agent independent central bus: eventbus + actionbus; clock/loader/orchestrator/plugins all connect.

orchestrator.description: Per-Agent conductor; CONTAINS the context-buffer. Event-driven frame: gather (plugins refresh blocks) → compose → llm.request{context,messages}. compose splits blocks by target: system blocks → system prompt text (priority DESC, wrapped <label>), message blocks → messages array (groups concatenated by priority DESC, within-group order kept; the conversation is one such block). Dispatch tool calls from llm.return.

loader.description: Per-Agent plugin lifecycle: load private(override)+public (independents are no-copy: code from public_plugin/, dataDir redirected agent-private), build v1.1 PluginContext (leveled log + print + log.entry push), inject llm library, register (setup) into event-system, teardown.

agent_instance.description: Wraps ONE Agent: holds+wires clock+event-system+orchestrator+loader; receives the global llm library; start()->loader loads->orchestrator conducts; stop().

boot.description: Global startup ONLY: read each agent's config + config/llm.json, build the global llm library, build + launch each agent_instance. Composition root.

cli.description: Global `krakey <command>` CLI: a top-level dispatcher (pure argv parser in dispatcher.ts) wrapping the interactive (@inquirer/prompts) config tool — bare/setup/agent/default/providers open the arrow-key pages to edit agent configs, the Default Setting, and the LLM communicator catalogue (Krakey logo); `krakey start` and `krakey dashboard` launch the boot runtime and the config-web Config console as CHILD PROCESSES (node --import tsx; never imported — cli's only contract is agent); plus help/version. Pure fs-ops live in shared/config-ops (index.ts is a re-export barrel) — shared with config-web.

config-web.description: Global standalone config management web UI: serves a static SPA + token-gated loopback JSON API (inspector-style auth); reads/writes the same files as cli via shared/config-ops; auto-renders every plugin's settings from the plugins' self-described configSchema (assembleSchema globs public_plugin/*/config-schema.ts) plus an onboarding wizard. Decoupled from the runtime; npm run config:web.

console.description: Global unified web console: a standalone static loopback server serving one shell (persistent top nav-bar + a Dashboard landing) that embeds the three web surfaces — config-web, the web chat channel, and the inspector — each in an iframe by URL. Holds no secrets and no API; surface URLs are configurable via env (defaults to the conventional loopback ports). Each embedded surface hides its own brand when framed so only the console's brand shows. Decoupled from the runtime; npm run console.

llm-gateway.description: Global LLM communication module: builds a key-less CommunicatorLibrary from config/llm.json (provider adapters: anthropic, openai-compatible; native fetch). Holds keys; does request+parse. Industry-standard infra, solidified into core.

llm-core.description: The LLM round-trip plugin: llm.request (carrying context + messages) -> communicator chat (key-less ctx.llm) -> llm.return (+ output.message for non-empty content — the monologue HOOK for observers, NOT a channel send). Reads the conversation from the event's messages (no conversation.get); emits llm.request.sent (the exact assembled request — system+messages+tools+params) for observers. Reads the registered tools from the tool-manager plugin via the llm.list_tools action when assembling each request (the llm.register_tool registry moved to tool-manager); requires:[llm.list_tools].

tool-manager.description: The per-Agent tool registry, extracted from llm-core so the round-trip and the tool plugins stay decoupled (neither imports the other). Owns the llm.register_tool action (same name tool plugins already call — add/replace a ToolDef by name, validated) and exposes llm.list_tools -> ToolDef[] (a fresh snapshot). provides:[llm.register_tool] so the loader sequences it before every tool plugin; llm-core requires:[llm.list_tools]. Holds no LLM/model behaviour; all state in the factory closure (one registry per Agent, R6).

persona.description: Stable identity context block (priority 10000+, stable-prefix convention) rendered from the agent's config.

system-prompt.description: Stable system-target context block (priority 9000, just below persona) introducing the LLM to the agent's OPERATING MODEL — the monologue rule + basic tool-use, CHANNEL-AGNOSTIC (never names a channel's send tool). Text/priority configurable. Identity lives in persona; this is operation.

web-chat.description: Browser chat channel: a refcounted module-level node:http server (one per process); POST /api/agents/:id/message -> input.message + clock.fire_now; SSE /api/agents/:id/stream streams ONLY what the agent explicitly sends via its web-chat.send_message tool (registered with llm-core; the orchestrator dispatches the call to web-chat's action) — the LLM's output.message monologue is NOT auto-streamed. Contributes a `web-chat.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 8000, text/priority config-overridable) teaching THIS channel's own usage (Web Chat is a message channel; reach its user only by calling web-chat.send_message) — the general monologue/operating-model rule stays in the separate `system-prompt` plugin. OWNS THE CONVERSATION via a `web-chat.conversation` message-block (priority 5000) rendering its persisted transcript as clean turns (user input + agent sends; no monologue, no tool mechanics). The data-carrying plugin, so private-by-default (per-agent transcript). per-message sent/read status (read on llm.return). Serves a dependency-free static chat page.

inspector.description: Read-only browser debug/analysis dashboard: own refcounted node:http hub (loopback + token) + per-agent SSE; subscribes to ALL well-known bus events into a bounded in-memory record ring (live + scrollback); serves a dependency-free page showing prompts sent (the actual assembled llm.request.sent — system+messages+tools+params — with a readable/raw toggle) / received (llm.return), the event timeline, and detailed logs (incl. core:* lines), correlated per-frame by request id. Emits nothing — never perturbs the observed agent.

krakeycode.description: Coding-agent toolkit: gives a Krakey Agent real computer-use tools — krakeycode.read_file / write_file / edit_file (exact-substring replace) / bash (shell via cp.spawn; SIGKILL timeout; byte-capped output) / list_dir — declared as ToolDefs via llm.register_tool. Dual-mode security (local default | sandbox: root-confined + optional command allowlist; command timeout cap in both). Contributes a krakeycode.guidance SYSTEM block (priority 7000) describing the tools + current mode + pointing Krakey at the docs/PLUGIN_DEV.md authoring guide, and a krakeycode.results MESSAGES block (priority 4000) that folds tool.result outcomes back into the conversation on the next frame (plain user/name:krakeycode turns, not tool-role pairing) and nudges clock.fire_now.

web-search.description: Web-search tool: gives a Krakey Agent a `web-search.search` tool (registered via llm.register_tool) that fetches results over native fetch with an AbortController timeout. Backend waterfall: an explicit config `instanceUrl` (a SearXNG instance) is authoritative when set; otherwise a local SearXNG (http://localhost:8080) is tried FIRST, then optional public SearXNG instances (publicInstances, default EMPTY — public SearXNG JSON is unreliable), then a keyless DuckDuckGo Lite fallback (useDuckDuckGoFallback, default true; HTML scraped) — the first backend returning results wins, all-fail throws. NEVER spawns containers; no persistence. Contributes a `web-search.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 6000) and a `web-search.results` MESSAGES block (priority 3500) folding recent search outcomes back into the conversation on the next frame (plain user/name:web-search turns; on failure it tells the agent to surface the error to the user) and nudging clock.fire_now.

browser.description: Browser-control tool: gives a Krakey Agent READ+NAVIGATE control of a Krakey-MANAGED Chrome via raw Chrome DevTools Protocol over Node's global WebSocket (ZERO deps). Chrome is spawned via child_process with --remote-debugging-port + a dedicated --user-data-dir profile under dataDir, launched LAZILY on the first tool call, reused, relaunched on crash, killed on teardown. Tools (registered via llm.register_tool): browser.navigate, browser.read_page (text|html, char-capped), browser.list_tabs (no auto-launch), browser.activate_tab, browser.screenshot (PNG written to dataDir/screenshots, returns path — the LLM envelope is text-only). NO click/type/eval. One CDP WebSocket multiplexes commands by id with per-command timeouts + flat sessions. Contributes a `browser.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 5500) and a `browser.results` MESSAGES block (priority 3000) folding tool.result outcomes back as plain user/name:browser turns on the next frame + nudging clock.fire_now. Mirrors the searxng/krakeycode tool-plugin shape.

memory-note.description: Krakey's private long-term notebook: gives a Krakey Agent `memory-note.remember` (save a note with a kind — goal|keep-in-mind|thought|finding — and an importance 1-5) and `memory-note.forget` (drop a note by id), declared as ToolDefs via llm.register_tool. The whole notebook is re-rendered into context EVERY frame as a `memory-note` SYSTEM block (priority 8500, grouped by kind, importance DESC) so the agent keeps it in mind; a config-overridable `memory-note.guidance` SYSTEM block (priority 6700) teaches the tools. Data-carrying, so private-by-default (per-agent notes.json under dataDir, bounded by maxNotes with LEAST-IMPORTANT eviction when full). No results block / no clock.fire_now — writes are synchronous and the notes block is itself the feedback.

history.description: Per-agent, persisted, compacting tool-use log. AUTO-CAPTURES the bus tool.result event (every tool, all plugins) AND exposes a history.record action other plugins may push richer entries to (it authors nothing on its own). Renders a compacted trail as a `history.log` SYSTEM block (priority 4500) so the agent sees what it has done; persists to history.json under its private dataDir, bounded by maxNotes-style compaction (keep `keepRecent`, drop the rest when over `maxEntries`). On each compaction it DISTILLS a checkpoint note into memory-note — declared dependency: manifest.requires=["memory-note.remember"], so the loader guarantees memory-note loads first. Volatile working buffer that hands durable milestones to [[memory-note]].

interval_toggle.description: Self-pacing tools: interval.set (change the stable frame rate) + interval.hold (run a different interval for the next N frames, then auto-revert to the base). Drives the per-Agent clock over the action bus (clock.set_interval / clock.set_default_interval, payload { ms }); counts a hold down on clock.tick and reverts via set_default_interval (set_interval is clobbered by the clock's per-frame reset). Contributes an interval.guidance SYSTEM block.

restart.description: Self-restart tool: restart.now re-execs the runtime so newly-written plugins / changed config (read only at startup) are applied. Spawns a detached replacement that waits delayMs (frees ports first), re-runs the launch command (node + execArgv + boot entry), then exits this process; dryRun reports the plan without restarting. Powerful — NOT in the default loadout. Contributes a restart.guidance SYSTEM block.

retry.description: Failure-reactive pacing: on a failed llm.return (ok:false) it shortens the frame interval (clock.set_interval, optional backoff) so the agent retries sooner; a successful return resets the streak. Self-contained — no requires, no context blocks, no context.full reaction; guards every clock action with actions.has.

logbook.description: Comprehensive activity log: subscribes to all well-known bus events + log.entry, records compact timestamped/typed summaries into a bounded ring AND a persistent per-agent activity.jsonl, and exposes a log.fetch tool (query by time window + type/level/ok) so the agent can self-diagnose failures. Folds fetch results back next frame; data-carrying (private).

config.description: OpenKrakeyConfig / DefaultAgentSetting / LLMConfig types + path constants.

config-ops.description: Pure fs-only config operations (Cli interface + createCli + CliError/CliParseError/normalizeBaseURL) shared by the cli and config-web tools. Extracted from packages/cli/src/index.ts (now a re-export barrel).

theme.description: Krakey brand primitives (mint/dim/bold/red painters, ✦ glyph, ✔/✖ verdict lines) shared by every console surface (cli, boot startup report); degrades plain on non-TTY/NO_COLOR.

http-auth.description: Per-process session-token primitives shared by the loopback HTTP plugins (web-chat, inspector): constant-time tokenOk (openWhenUnset knob) + Bearer/query/cookie extractors (cookie decode knob, fail-closed). Plugins keep their own source precedence + token-from-config policy.
